title: Kaboom Crew — networked play (connected profile, two browser tabs share one arena)
summary: Land the network track for Kaboom Crew: two browser tabs share a single live arena via the AGF backend-agnostic protocol (M20 epic). First implementable shape: lockstep + input rollback on top of the existing fixed-step simulation, served by the Node reference backend. Driven by GDP-007 (Networked Kaboom Crew). Out of scope for MVP-1 and MVP-2 — this epic is its own multi-sprint track.
doneCriteria: Two browser tabs hitting the same room id see the same arena state
Inputs from both tabs reach both simulations within ~1 RTT
A disconnect from one tab pauses the round (not the whole sim) and resumes on reconnect
notes: The dev-bridge POST /__agf/commands and POST /__agf/input/action surfaces are NOT the multiplayer surface — they're dev-only.
Real multiplayer goes through the protocol contract under engine/protocol/ which already has Node + .NET reference backends.
